notes: Step-by-step diagnostic procedures for common E65 problems. Use these as systematic troubleshooting guides.

procedures.title: No start / crank but no fire

steps.action: Check battery voltage with the OBD app. Must be 11.5V+ for crank, 12.4V at rest.
steps.if_fail: Charge or replace battery. Register new battery if replaced.

steps.action: Listen for fuel pump prime when key is turned to position II. 2-3 second whine from rear.
steps.if_fail: Check fuel pump fuse F109 (engine bay E-box). Then fuel pump relay. Then pump itself.

steps.action: Read DTCs via OBD app. Look for P0335 (crank sensor), P0606 (DME fault), or fuel-pressure codes.
steps.if_fail: Address codes before continuing.

steps.action: Try spare key fob if you have one. E65 has known CAS module receiver issues.
steps.if_fail: ELV (steering lock) failure is common — needs ISTA diagnosis.

steps.action: If cranks but no spark — pull a coil and visually check spark on a grounded plug.
steps.if_fail: Could be timing chain skip — check P0016/P0017 codes.

steps.action: Read DTCs. Look for P0301-P0306 (specific cylinder) or P0300 (random).
If specific cylinder code: swap that coil with another cylinder, clear codes, drive 5 min.
steps.if_fail: If misfire follows the coil to new cylinder = coil bad. Replace.

steps.action: If misfire stays on original cylinder after coil swap = spark plug or injector or compression issue.
steps.if_fail: Replace plug first (€10). Then injector swap test.

steps.action: If P0300 random: check valve cover gasket for oil in plug wells.
steps.if_fail: Replace valve cover gasket — universal N52 issue at 100k km.

steps.action: Check fuel pressure (live data screen). Should be 3.5-4.0 bar at idle.
steps.if_fail: Low fuel pressure — check pump and filter.

steps.action: Look at fuel trims (live data). LTFT > +15% = lean condition (vacuum leak).
steps.if_fail: Smoke test intake for vacuum leaks — CCV is the prime suspect.

steps.action: Park immediately if temp gauge in red. Engine damage starts within seconds at 120°C+.
Wait 30 min minimum before opening expansion tank cap.
steps.if_fail: Risk of severe burns from pressurized steam.

steps.action: Check coolant level when cold. Top up with G48 blue ONLY if needed.
steps.if_fail: Coolant low without obvious leak = head gasket suspect.

steps.action: Inspect expansion tank for cracks (very common E65).
steps.if_fail: Replace tank €80.

steps.action: Inspect under car for puddles. Front = radiator/hoses. Middle = water pump.
steps.if_fail: Pinpoint leak source.

steps.action: Open cap (when cold), start engine, watch coolant in tank. Should circulate after thermostat opens (~5 min).
steps.if_fail: No flow = electric water pump dead. Standard 100-120k km failure.

steps.action: Check radiator fan operation when A/C is on or temp climbs.
steps.if_fail: Fan not running = fan motor or controller fault. €400+ for new fan.

steps.action: Check iDrive → Settings → Tone → Balance and Fader. Make sure both centred.
steps.if_fail: Pull settings to default.

steps.action: Check fuse F53 in boot fuse box (30A HiFi amplifier).
steps.if_fail: If blown, replace €1. Test radio.

steps.action: Read battery voltage with engine running. Must be 13.8-14.4V.
steps.if_fail: Weak alternator can starve amp.

steps.action: Check for one-speaker-only working — usually driver-left. That's the CCC backup amp; main DSP amp dead.
steps.if_fail: Main amp needs rebuild. See fault 'logic7_amp_dead' entry.

steps.action: Pull LEFT boot trim (Logic 7 amp lives in left rear corner per BMW Training). Locate amp, check connectors firmly seated.
steps.if_fail: Loose connector = common easy fix.

steps.action: Look at amp for water damage signs (corrosion, white residue).
steps.if_fail: Water-killed amp — replace, find boot seal leak source.

steps.action: Read DTCs via OBD app's Fault Codes screen.
Note ALL codes including pending (Mode 07). Multiple related codes point to root cause.
Tap each code in the app to read description and DIY rating.
Check Freeze Frame data — engine conditions when fault was set.
steps.if_fail: Helps diagnose intermittent faults.

steps.action: Common culprits on N52: VANOS solenoid, CCV/oil separator, valve cover gasket leak.
steps.if_fail: Tackle highest-severity DTC first.

steps.action: Clear codes after repair. If they return within 50km — repair not complete.
steps.if_fail: Re-investigate.

procedures.title: Battery dies overnight or after short parking

steps.action: Check current draw with multimeter on negative terminal. Should be < 50mA after 30 min.
If high parasitic draw, pull fuses one at a time and watch ammeter for drop.
Common culprits on E65: CCC head unit not sleeping (HDD issue), comfort access antennas, trunk module.
Check that all doors are FULLY closed (microswitches). One open switch = car never sleeps.
steps.if_fail: Adjust or replace door switch.

steps.action: Test battery load (specialist). If failing despite good charging = replace + register.
steps.if_fail: Always register new battery via ISTA.

steps.action: Use full module scan in Fault Codes. Check DSC module specifically.
Look for codes 5DF0-5DF3 (wheel speed sensors).
steps.if_fail: Replace failed wheel speed sensor €60 + 1h labour.

steps.action: Check 5E20/5E21 codes (yaw/lateral accel sensors internal to DSC).
steps.if_fail: DSC unit replacement needed (specialist).

steps.action: Verify brake fluid level + condition.
steps.if_fail: Low fluid = leak. Old fluid = service due.

procedures.title: Car sits low at rear / air suspension fault

steps.action: Park overnight, check if rear has dropped significantly by morning.
steps.if_fail: Slow leak somewhere.

steps.action: Listen for compressor running when starting in the morning (catching up the leak).
steps.if_fail: Compressor running > 1 min = leak in system.

steps.action: Spray soapy water on air springs (above rear shock). Watch for bubbles.
steps.if_fail: Air bag leak — replace bag €300 each side.

steps.action: Check air lines for cracks near connectors.
steps.if_fail: Line cracking common in GCC heat.

steps.action: If no leak and compressor still constant = compressor brushes worn or pump failing.
steps.if_fail: Replace compressor €400 OEM.
